description: Migrate from Puppeteer to unified Playwright E2E testing. Add 7-layer testing pyramid: smoke, flows, cross-browser, accessibility, performance, visual regression, and synthetic monitoring. All 100% free via GitHub Actions.

userStories.title: Unified Playwright config + auth setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a single Playwright config with role-based auth setup so that all E2E tests run through one framework
acceptanceCriteria: tests/e2e/playwright.config.ts created with smoke, gestionnaire, prestataire, locataire, multi-role projects
tests/e2e/setup/auth.setup.ts created reusing existing Supabase GoTrue auth pattern from qa-bot
npm scripts updated: test:e2e:pw for Playwright, old test:e2e preserved temporarily
Config supports CI mode (1 worker, blob reporter) and local mode (html reporter, webServer)

userStories.title: Smoke tests (Couche 1)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want fast smoke tests that verify auth and navigation for all roles so that broken deploys are caught in < 2 min
acceptanceCriteria: Tests: gestionnaire dashboard, prestataire dashboard, locataire dashboard, public pages (landing, login), API health check
All tests use storageState (no UI login)
Tests pass locally against running dev server

userStories.title: Migrate property tests (building + lot creation)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the building and lot creation E2E tests migrated to Playwright so they use stable locators and auto-waiting
acceptanceCriteria: tests/e2e/pages/building-wizard.page.ts POM migrated to Playwright locators
tests/e2e/pages/lot-wizard.page.ts POM migrated to Playwright locators
All Puppeteer patterns replaced (waitForSelector→locator, page.type→fill, etc.)

userStories.title: Migrate contract + billing tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want contract and billing E2E tests migrated to Playwright

userStories.title: Migrate intervention tests (4 files)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all intervention E2E tests migrated to Playwright with proper multi-role support

userStories.title: CI/CD pipeline — main workflow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a CI pipeline that runs lint, typecheck, build, smoke, E2E, a11y, and perf tests on every PR
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml updated with full pipeline: lint → typecheck → build → e2e-smoke → e2e-flows
e2e-flows uses matrix strategy for 4 role projects
Playwright browsers cached for faster CI
Environment secrets referenced for E2E credentials

userStories.title: Accessibility tests (Couche 4)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want automated WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility scanning on all role-specific pages to catch a11y regressions
acceptanceCriteria: axe-core results attached as JSON artifacts for debugging
Tests run and produce results (violations documented, not necessarily zero)

userStories.title: Performance budget tests (Couche 5)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want performance budget tests that measure Core Web Vitals and fail if budgets are exceeded
acceptanceCriteria: Measures FCP (Paint Timing API), LCP (PerformanceObserver), CLS (Layout Instability API)
Budgets defined for 5 key pages with CI-adjusted thresholds
Metrics attached as JSON artifacts for trend tracking
Tests run and produce metrics (budgets may need adjustment after first run)

userStories.title: Visual regression tests (Couche 6)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want visual regression tests that compare component screenshots against baselines
acceptanceCriteria: 5 component-level screenshots: login, sidebar, intervention-list, pricing-cards, landing-hero
Dynamic content masked (timestamps, avatars, notification counts)
Baseline screenshots generated on first run

userStories.title: Nightly + monitoring CI workflows
userStories.description: As a developer, I want nightly cross-browser tests and periodic production smoke tests to catch issues not found in daily CI
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/nightly.yml runs cross-browser matrix (firefox, webkit, mobile-chrome, mobile-safari, tablet)
Monitoring tests are read-only (no data mutations)
Slack webhook notification on monitoring failure

userStories.title: Cleanup — remove Puppeteer infrastructure
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Puppeteer removed so we have a single test framework with no duplicate infrastructure
acceptanceCriteria: npm scripts updated: test:e2e now runs Playwright
No remaining references to puppeteer in codebase
